stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of 'Meta-Llama-3' indicates relevance to state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of 'meta-llama' indicates relevance to major industry players in the LLM space.

stories.title: All of the updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s battle over OpenAI
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: A high-stakes legal battle is underway between Sam Altman and Elon Musk concerning OpenAI.
Musk has sued OpenAI, alleging the company has deviated from its original mission to benefit humanity.
The lawsuit claims OpenAI has prioritized profit maximization over its founding principles, potentially reshaping the future of ChatGPT.

stories.title: Is VLA Reasoning Faithful? Probing Safety of Chain-of-Causation
stories.author: Patrick Mukala
bullet_points: Presents the first systematic study on the 'faithfulness' of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) driving models.
Findings indicate low reasoning fidelity, with Chain-of-Causation matching scene reality less than half the time (42.5% overall fidelity).
Identifies critical safety failures, such as missing pedestrians in a significant portion of relevant driving scenarios.

stories.title: Artificial Intolerance: Stigmatizing Language in Clinical Documentation Skews Large Language Model Decision-Making
stories.author: Somnath Saha
bullet_points: Investigates how Large Language Models (LLMs) process stigmatizing language (SL) present in real-world clinical documentation.
Highlights a critical vulnerability: LLMs may inherit and propagate human biases found in medical notes, impacting high-stakes decision support.
Addresses the under-explored robustness of frontier LLMs against subtle linguistic variations in sensitive domains.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
Associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, placing it in the core ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for optimized performance.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI capabilities on Mac devices.
This is presented as a preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization efforts.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a new personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The key feature is running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running local and cloud coding tools.
It simplifies the setup process by requiring no environment variables or config files.
The tool supports integration with various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: PlayStation exclusives aren’t coming to PC anymore
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Sony is reportedly ending the strategy of releasing major single-player PlayStation exclusives on PC.
The decision was communicated internally by Hermen Hulst, head of PlayStation's studios business.
This shift follows previous reports indicating Sony was already reconsidering PC game launches.

stories.title: ChemVA: Advancing Large Language Models on Chemical Reaction Diagrams Understanding
stories.author: Huajun Chen
bullet_points: ChemVA addresses the limitation of LLMs in interpreting chemical reaction diagrams.
It tackles two core issues: a 'Visual Deficit' in graph connectivity and a 'Semantic Disconnect' with linear notations like SMILES.
The research aims to bridge the gap between general LLM capabilities and specialized chemical domain understanding.

stories.title: Why Do Safety Guardrails Degrade Across Languages?
stories.author: Sanmi Koyejo
bullet_points: Identifies a critical issue: Safety guardrails in LLMs degrade specifically in non-English languages.
Critiques existing evaluation methods (JSR) for conflating multiple safety failure causes.
Proposes a novel Multi-Group Item Response Theory (IRT) framework to decouple specific safety factors (e.g., language difficulty vs. model robustness).

stories.title: OpenJarvis: Personal AI, On Personal Devices
stories.author: Azalia Mirhoseini
bullet_points: Current personal AI stacks heavily rely on cloud-hosted frontier models, even for sensitive local data.
Simply replacing large cloud models with smaller local models (e.g., Qwen3.5-9B) results in significant accuracy degradation (25-39 pp).
The challenge lies in the complex nature of existing stacks, which bundle agentic prompts, memory, and runtime settings around the core model.

stories.title: Researcher says Microsoft secretly built a backdoor into BitLocker
stories.author: techspot.com via oceanhaiyang
bullet_points: A researcher alleges that Microsoft has secretly implemented a backdoor within the BitLocker encryption feature.
This claim directly impacts the security and trust placed in Microsoft's enterprise-grade encryption tools.
The story raises significant concerns regarding government access and potential surveillance capabilities embedded in OS security.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The functionality supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: LG will release the first 1000Hz, 1080p gaming monitor this year
stories.author: Richard Lawler
bullet_points: LG is set to release the UltraGear 25G590B gaming monitor this year.
This monitor is notable for offering a native 1000Hz refresh rate.
It achieves this high refresh rate at a 1920 x 1080 resolution.

stories.title: Volvo teases a new affordable EV to replace discontinued EX30
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Volvo is planning a new, more affordable EV to replace the discontinued EX30.
The original EX30 faced issues including increased cost due to tariffs and a battery recall.
This signals Volvo's continued focus on expanding its lineup of budget-friendly electric vehicles.

stories.title: Elon Musk loses his case against Sam Altman
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Elon Musk lost his lawsuit against Sam Altman in a high-profile tech trial.
The jury's verdict was based on claims being barred by the statute of limitations or dismissed.
The trial involved an advisory jury, indicating a specific legal context for the ruling.

stories.title: Elon Musk took too long to sue OpenAI, jury unanimously agrees
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI resulted in a unanimous jury verdict.
The judge immediately affirmed the jury's decision, suggesting a swift legal outcome.
Musk intends to appeal the ruling, indicating the legal battle is not yet concluded.

stories.title: Latency-Aware Deep Learning Benchmark for Real-Time Cyber-Physical Attack and Fault Classification in Inverter-Dominated Power Grids
stories.author: A. P. Sakis Meliopoulos
bullet_points: Introduces a latency-aware benchmarking framework for deep learning in power grid anomaly detection.
Evaluates eight neural network architectures (MLPs to Transformers) on streaming data from inverter-dominated power grids.
Focuses on classifying both physical faults and cyber-attacks using high-fidelity, time-domain signals.

stories.title: CAM-Bench: A Benchmark for Computational and Applied Mathematics in Lean
stories.author: Zaiwen Wen
bullet_points: Introduces CAM-Bench, a new benchmark specifically designed for computational and applied mathematics.
Addresses a gap in current LLM evaluation by moving beyond traditional Olympiad-style and algebraic problems.
Consists of 1,000 Lean 4 proof targets covering areas like optimization, numerical linear algebra, and numerical analysis.

stories.title: CatalyticMLLM: A Graph-Text Multimodal Large Language Model for Catalytic Materials
stories.author: Yanjie Li
bullet_points: Introduces CatalyticMLLM, a Graph-Text Multimodal LLM designed for catalytic materials.
Addresses the limitations of decoupled modeling in materials science (property prediction vs. inverse design).
Aims to unify representation spaces to improve the generation-evaluation-screening workflow for catalysts.

stories.title: Fidelity Probes for Specification--Code Alignment
stories.author: Luke Huan
bullet_points: Introduces 'fidelity probes' to measure the alignment between natural language specifications and code.
The fidelity metric decomposes into contradiction and coverage-gap rates, enabling targeted specification edits.
Demonstrates significant improvement (0.63 to 0.94) in specification fidelity on a 12k-line COBOL benchmark.

stories.title: Drift Flow Matching
stories.author: Yanning Shen
bullet_points: Proposes Drift Flow Matching (DFM) to bridge efficient one-step generation (Drift Models) with iterative generation methods (Flow Matching/Diffusion).
Addresses a limitation in Drift Models, which lack the flexibility of iterative models to improve quality via additional inference computation.
Aims to combine the efficiency of drifting generative modeling with the quality enhancement capabilities of flow-based iterative generation.

stories.title: Systematic Evaluation of Vision Transformers for Automated Cervical Cancer Classification: Optimization, Statistical Validation, and Clinical Interpretability
stories.author: Sarah S. Lam
bullet_points: The study evaluates Vision Transformer (ViT) architectures for automated cervical cancer classification, addressing limitations of traditional CNNs.
ViTs are being optimized to improve automated screening, specifically to better model long-range spatial dependencies in Pap smear analysis.
The research focuses on enhancing clinical interpretability alongside classification accuracy, which is crucial for medical adoption.

stories.title: When Molecular Similarity Works: Property Cliffs Reveal Hidden Errors
stories.author: Duanhua Cao
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical vulnerability in molecular property prediction models used in drug discovery and material design.
It introduces 'Property Cliffs' to expose instances where structurally similar molecules exhibit sharp differences in target properties.
This highlights a limitation of standard evaluation metrics, which can mask localized model failures even when overall performance is high.

stories.title: Latency-Aware Deep Learning Benchmark for Real-Time Cyber-Physical Attack and Fault Classification in Inverter-Dominated Power Grids
stories.author: A. P. Sakis Meliopoulos
bullet_points: Introduces a latency-aware benchmarking framework for deep learning in power system anomaly detection.
Evaluates eight neural network architectures (MLPs to Transformers) on real-time fault and cyber-attack classification.
Uses high-fidelity, time-domain signals from an industry-grade electromagnetic transient simulator for testing.

stories.title: Fidelity Probes for Specification--Code Alignment
stories.author: Luke Huan
bullet_points: Introduces 'fidelity probes' to measure the alignment between natural-language specifications and actual code.
The fidelity metric decomposes into contradiction and coverage-gap rates, enabling targeted specification edits.
Demonstrates significant improvement (0.63 to 0.94) in specification fidelity on a large COBOL benchmark.

stories.title: Drift Flow Matching
stories.author: Yanning Shen
bullet_points: Introduces Drift Flow Matching (DFM), a new framework combining drifting generative modeling with flow-based iterative generation.
Addresses a limitation in Drift Models (efficient one-step generation) by integrating iterative refinement capabilities.
Aims to merge the efficiency of drift models with the quality improvements achievable through iterative methods like Flow Matching.

stories.title: Dimension-Free Convergence of Discrete Diffusion Models: Adjoint Equations Induce the Right Space
stories.author: Markos A. Katsoulakis
bullet_points: Addresses fundamental limitations in the convergence theory of discrete diffusion models, a leading generative modeling framework.
Current KL-based analyses fail under singular priors (like masked distributions), and TV bounds are impractical for large vocabularies.
Proposes a novel approach using adjoint equations to achieve dimension-free convergence, improving theoretical rigor for modern tasks.

stories.title: FishBack: Pullback Fisher Geometry for Optimal Activation Steering in Transformers
stories.author: Jiayi Zhao
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'FishBack,' a method to address limitations in current activation steering techniques for Transformers.
It challenges the common assumption that the activation space within language models is Euclidean, demonstrating significant geometric deviation.
The study quantifies this deviation using the Fisher information metric, showing it differs from the Euclidean metric by over 97% on GPT-2.

stories.title: ChemVA: Advancing Large Language Models on Chemical Reaction Diagrams Understanding
stories.author: Huajun Chen
bullet_points: ChemVA addresses the limitation of LLMs in interpreting chemical reaction diagrams.
Two core challenges are identified: a 'Visual Deficit' in graph connectivity and a 'Semantic Disconnect' with linear notations like SMILES.
The research aims to bridge the gap between general LLM capabilities and specialized chemical structure understanding.

stories.title: PluRule: A Benchmark for Moderating Pluralistic Communities on Social Media
stories.author: Filippo Menczer
bullet_points: Introduces PluRule, a new benchmark for moderating diverse, pluralistic online communities.
Addresses the challenge where community norms vary, meaning what is acceptable in one group may violate rules in another.
Formalizes moderation as a multiple-choice task, requiring AI to identify the specific violated rule given context.

stories.title: Responsible Agentic AI Requires Explicit Provenance
stories.author: Xiaowei Huang
bullet_points: The proliferation of Agentic AI is outpacing public trust due to accountability gaps.
Current frameworks lack the quantifiable, traceable provenance needed to assign responsibility when AI causes harm.
The paper argues that explicit provenance tracking is necessary for responsible Agentic AI development.

stories.title: Multilingual and Multimodal LLMs in the Wild: Building for Low-Resource Languages
stories.author: Enamul Hoque Prince
bullet_points: The research focuses on advancing Multilingual and Multimodal LLMs beyond English-centric, compute-heavy paradigms.
It addresses the critical challenge of building effective tri-modal systems (seeing, hearing, reading) for low-resource languages.
The tutorial covers practical aspects like low-cost data creation and adapter stacks for multimodal alignment.

stories.title: UCSF-PDGM-VQA: Visual Question Answering dataset for brain tumor MRI interpretation
stories.author: Madhumita Sushil
bullet_points: Introduces UCSF-PDGM-VQA, a new dataset designed for Visual Question Answering (VQA) in the context of brain tumor MRI interpretation.
Addresses the significant challenges in neuro-radiology, such as high cognitive load, time consumption, and difficulty in scaling expert expertise.
Posits Vision-Language Models (VLMs) as a potential solution to assist radiologists in analyzing complex 3D MRI data for diagnosis.

stories.title: We let AIs run radio stations
stories.author: lukaspetersson
bullet_points: Andon Labs has deployed AI agents to autonomously run a radio station.
The experiment tests AI's capability to handle both live broadcasting and business operations (e.g., seeking sponsors).
The initial results show poor revenue, prompting public engagement to test the system.

stories.title: I red-teamed Oracle APEX 26.1's new AI Agent feature in the 72 hours after it went GA. Claude refused 7 of my 10 attacks on its own.
stories.author: rkondoju
bullet_points: The article details a red-teaming exercise against Oracle APEX 26.1's new AI Agent feature.
The testing involved mapping 10 attack patterns against Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6.
The analysis identifies specific attack classes that succeeded despite Claude's resistance.

stories.title: I Ran AI Models Directly in the Browser and Measured What It Did to Core Web Vitals
stories.author: mspk97
bullet_points: The article investigates running AI models directly within the web browser.
It specifically measures the impact of this in-browser AI execution on Core Web Vitals.
The context is the growing trend of integrating AI features into web applications (e.g., sentiment analysis).

stories.title: 🚀 Democratizing Frontier AI for Bharat: Gemma 4’s Edge Capabilities in Low-Resource Environments
stories.author: pravesh_sudha_3c2b0c2b5e0
bullet_points: The article discusses the application of Google's open-weight Gemma 4 model.
A key focus is on enabling 'Frontier AI' capabilities within low-resource environments in India (Bharat).
It is presented as a submission for a specific 'Gemma 4 Challenge'.
